D7 Print L4 Analyze Reference

Screen Angles & the Rosette

offset the four process screens by 30° and they weave a tight rosette — line them up and an ugly moiré blooms across the page

Four overlaid halftone screens would clash badly if printed at the same angle. The standard fix spaces the strong inks 30° apart — cyan 15°, black 45°, magenta 75° — with weak yellow at 90°. That offset turns the overlap into a small, stable rosette instead of a large low-frequency moiré. Inks stack subtractively (multiply), not by addition.

Drag the cyan screen off its 15° angle and toward black (45°) or magenta (75°). As the gap to a neighbour shrinks, the rosette dissolves into broad moiré bands. Hide yellow to see the three structural inks more clearly.
